title: NIS2 and Your SOC: A Practical Compliance Checklist
meta_title: NIS2 SOC Requirements Checklist | Nordic SOC
meta_description: A practical checklist of NIS2 SOC requirements: scope, risk management, incident reporting, supply chain and evidence, plus how an AI SOC helps you comply.
excerpt: What does NIS2 require of your SOC? A practical checklist covering scope, risk-management measures, incident reporting, supply-chain security and evidence, and how an AI SOC supports faster detection and accurate, audit-ready reporting.
author: Nordic SOC
hero_image_alt: Security operations centre dashboard showing NIS2 compliance monitoring and incident reporting

faq.q: What does NIS2 require of a SOC?
faq.a: NIS2 expects in-scope organisations to operate risk-based security measures, detect incidents promptly, handle and recover from them, and notify the national CSIRT or competent authority of significant incidents within defined windows. A SOC delivers these outcomes through continuous monitoring, alert triage, investigation, response, and audit-ready evidence.

faq.q: When did NIS2 come into force?
faq.a: NIS2 was adopted as an EU directive in 2022. Member states transposed it into national law, with a transposition deadline of October 2024. Because it is a directive rather than a regulation, the exact rules, deadlines, and classifications vary by member state, so organisations should confirm their obligations under each relevant national law.

faq.q: What is the difference between essential and important entities under NIS2?
faq.a: Both essential and important entities must meet comparable risk-management and reporting obligations. The main difference is supervision: essential entities face proactive, ongoing oversight, while important entities are generally supervised reactively, after an incident or indication of non-compliance. Classification depends on sector and organisation size under national law.

faq.q: How does NIS2 incident reporting work?
faq.a: For significant incidents, NIS2 uses a phased model: an early warning shortly after the organisation becomes aware of the incident, a fuller notification with an initial assessment, and a final report later covering root cause and mitigation. The precise timeframes are set in each member state's transposition of the directive.

faq.q: How does an AI SOC help with NIS2 compliance?
faq.a: An AI SOC accelerates detection and triage, automates investigation to determine whether an incident is significant, and generates structured, time-stamped records as it works. That documentation supports both incident notifications and supervisory audits, helping organisations meet NIS2's demands for speed, consistency, and evidence at scale.
